channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump critic Mark Carney poised to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney’s challenge will be to differentiate himself from Trudeau and take on Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre. If Carney wins he's expected to call for an early general election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Widespread killing, rape, disease and war make Democratic Republic of Congo hell on earth
item.description: Fighting by Islamic State and other terrorist groups over minerals and power has led to widespread killings and rape in the DRC. Disease, child slavery and Christian persecution stalk the population.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — After weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism, Panama on Saturday released dozens of migrants who were held for weeks in a remote camp after being deported from the United States, telling them they have 30 days to leave the Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: BY MEGAN JANETSKY, ALMA SOLÍS and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: They were forced to scam others worldwide. Now thousands are held in detention on the Myanmar border
item.description: Thousands of sick, exhausted and terrified young men and women, from countries all over the world squat in rows, packed shoulder to shoulder, surgical masks covering their mouths and, some, their eyes.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu, Jintamas Saksornchai And Martha Mendoza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian forces walked inside a gas pipeline to strike Ukrainian troops from the rear in Kursk
item.description: Ukraine’s military and Russian war bloggers say Russian special forces walked inside a gas pipeline to strike Ukrainian units from the rear in the Kursk region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Honduras holds primaries as voter frustration simmers over security and the economy
item.description: TEGUCIGALPA, Honduras (AP) — Voters in Honduras will select candidates from the three main parties Sunday to compete in November’s general election for the presidency in a country that remains deeply polarized, but skeptical of leaders from the left and right who have failed to deliver on improving
item.dc:creator: MARLON GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh brace for upcoming food reductions as aid agencies cut funding
item.description: COX’S BAZAR, Bangladesh (AP) — Rohingya refugees in crammed Bangladeshi camps say they are worried about a U.S.
item.dc:creator: SHAFIQUR RAHMAN and JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran's supreme leader rejects nuclear talks with US after Trump's overtures
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader, said in a series of X posts Saturday he would not negotiate a nuclear deal with the U.S. due to its demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish prime minister warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Impeached South Korean leader freed from prison
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison Saturday after a court ruled his detention over insurrection charges was invalid. The conservative president faces charges for his December effort to impose martial law that thrust the country into chaos.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Gaza plan criticized by US and Israel gets European support
item.description: Foreign ministers from France, Germany, Italy and the UK announced their support for Egypt's Gaza reconstruction plan, despite Israel and the US expressing reservations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan legislation seeks to rein in Erdogan's Turkey over ties to US foes
item.description: A bipartisan group of congressmen have taken issue with NATO ally Turkey aligning its foreign policy interests against the U.S. and its Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol freed from prison after canceled arrest
item.description: South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday after a court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting in Canada leaves a dozen wounded, police say
item.description: A shooting Friday evening at a pub in Toronto, Canada, left a dozen people wounded, as police continue to search for the suspect, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare cyclone weakens to a tropical low weather system as it approaches the Australian east coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — A tropical cyclone weakened to a tropical low weather system on Saturday as it approached Brisbane, Australia’s third-most populous city, bringing flooding rain that was expected to lash the coastal region for days.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 90,000 Palestinians attend the first Friday prayers of Ramadan in Jerusalem
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — In the first Friday prayers of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan, about 90,000 Palestinians prayed at the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in Jerusalem’s Old City under tight security by Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: IMAD ISSEID and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization after giving world a sign of his frailty
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis hit the three-week mark Friday in his hospitalization for double pneumonia, in stable condition, receiving therapy but not necessarily improving, after giving the world a tangible indication of just how frail and sick he is.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and MARIA CHENG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls it 'more difficult' to deal with Ukraine than Russia
item.description: President Trump on Friday warned that the U.S. would cut off its support for Ukraine if it did not agree to settle its war against Russia, telling reporters it has been “more difficult” to deal with Kyiv than with Moscow. "I think we're doing very well with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: The UN is reporting that four boats with immigrants from East Africa capsized off of Yemen leaving 186 missing. Two crewmembers were rescued.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis still receiving oxygen therapy, will not read Sunday prayers: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still receiving oxygen therapy and will not be able to read Sunday prayers, according to the Vatican. The pontiff is now entering his fourth week in Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN prepping for spending cuts as DOGE roots out waste in US, internal docs show
item.description: Fox News Digital obtained an internal United Nations memo indicating that the international body is preparing heads of departments for funding cuts as DOGE roots out waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boy with loaded shotgun boarded plane in Australia, tackled by passenger
item.description: A 17-year-old boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane at an Australian airport before being restrained by a pilot and two passengers, police said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Olympic snowboarder now on FBI's 10 most wanted list
item.description: Former Olympic snowboarder Ryan Wedding was added to the FBI’s 10 most wanted list Thursday for allegedly running a transnational drug trafficking network.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the mi
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: One moment, calm waters. The next, a 900-pound dolphin landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
